mnemonics: CLI GUI SOUP (Command Line, Graphical User, System calls, OS, User, Programs) - Covers the main ways a user interacts with the OS.
keyPoints: The OS provides interfaces like CLI and GUI for user interaction.
System calls are the programming interface that allows programs to request services from the OS.
The shell is a command-line interpreter that acts as an interface between the user and the OS kernel.
commonMistakes: Confusing the shell (e.g., Bash) with the kernel itself; the shell is just a program that uses the kernel.
Believing the GUI is the OS; it is merely a user-friendly program running on top of the OS.
Assuming all user commands are directly handled by the kernel; most are processed by system utilities.
